result.summary: Fivetran exposes a REST API (including unauthenticated public connector-metadata endpoints) and a self-hosted MCP server package that uses Fivetran API credentials; OAuth endpoints for an `mcp` scope are present but no public developer-facing OAuth integration surface was documented.
result.description: Fivetran is a managed data movement platform that syncs data from many source systems into cloud data warehouses and lakes. It provides tools for managing connectors, destinations, transformations, and related account infrastructure.

fivetran_user_api_key.label: Fivetran scoped user API key and secret
fivetran_user_api_key.setup: In the Fivetran dashboard, open [User API Config](https://fivetran.com/dashboard/user/api-config) to create a scoped API key tied to your user account. Use the resulting key and secret together; the docs say requests use the Base64-encoded key and secret, and access is limited by your RBAC permissions. The REST API getting started guide recommends a scoped API key.

fivetran_system_key.label: Fivetran system key and secret
fivetran_system_key.setup: Create a system key either in the dashboard at [System Keys](https://fivetran.com/docs/getting-started/fivetran-dashboard/account-settings/system-keys) via **Account settings** → **General** → **System Keys**, or via the REST API's [Create System Key](https://fivetran.com/docs/rest-api/api-reference/system-key/create-system-key) endpoint. System keys can only be managed with user keys, and the user must have the Account Administrator role. Use the key and secret together.

fivetran_oauth_app.label: Fivetran OAuth app/client for MCP scope
fivetran_oauth_app.setup: Fivetran exposes OAuth endpoints at `https://fivetran.com/oauth/authorize` and `https://fivetran.com/oauth/token`, with the `mcp` scope detected by automated probes. I could not find public developer documentation describing where a developer registers an OAuth client/app or how this is intended to be acquired.
